en: Is there a connection? There has to be.
Well, I doubt it since she's been dead for 14 years.
Technically, she was never alive.
ko: 기술적으로 그녀는 한 번도 살아있지 않았어.

en: Rachel was Jennifer Wilson's stillborn daughter 14 years ago.
Oh, that's...
That's not right.
Why would she do that? Why?
Why would she think of her daughter in her last moments?
Yup, sociopath. I'm seeing it now.
She didn't think about her daughter.
She scratched her name on the floor with her fingernails.
She was dying. It took effort. It would have hurt.
ko: 그녀는 죽어가고 있었어. 힘이 들었을 거야. 아팠을 거야.

en: You said that the victims all took the poison themselves,
ko: 너는 피해자들이 모두 스스로 독을 마셨다고 했지,

en: that he makes them take it.
Well, maybe he, I don't know, talks to them?
ko: 글쎄, 아마 그가, 잘 모르겠지만, 그들과 대화했을까?

en: Maybe he used the death of her daughter somehow.
ko: 아마 그녀의 딸의 죽음을 뭔가에 이용했을지도 몰라.

en: But that was ages ago. Why would she still be upset?
ko: 하지만 그건 오래 전 일이야. 왜 아직도 괴로워할까?

en: Not good? Bit not good, yeah.
ko: 좋지 않아? 조금 좋지 않아, 그래.

en: Yeah, but if you were dying,
if you'd been murdered in your very last few seconds,
what would you say? Please, God, let me live.
ko: 너는 뭐라고 말할까? 제발, 신이여, 살게 해주세요.

en: Use your imagination.
I don't have to.
Yeah, but if you were clever, really clever...
ko: 응, 하지만 만약 네가 똑똑하다면, 정말 똑똑하다면...
